title: wedelia procumbens
intro: Wedelia procumbens, also known as the creeping wedelia or dwarf daisy, is a perennial herb belonging to the sunflower family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Wedelia procumbens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried for various herbal preparations.
